_docs.usage: Add GitHub repos to 'repos' array. Run: npm run sync-projects

commands.npm run sync:generate: Generate .md files from cache using claude CLI
commands.npm run sync-projects: Run both steps above in sequence
commands.npm run sync:generate -- --force=slug: Overwrite a specific non-locked project file

repo_fields.order (required): Unique integer. Existing projects use 1-3, so start at 4+
repo_fields.lock: Set true to prevent regeneration. --force will NOT overwrite locked repos.
repo_fields.overrides.title: Custom display title (default: title-case of repo name)
repo_fields.overrides.slug: Custom URL slug (default: kebab-case of repo name)
repo_fields.overrides.description: Custom description (default: AI-generated from repo content)
repo_fields.overrides.tags: Array of strings (default: AI-generated from repo content)
repo_fields._note: links.github is always auto-set from the repo URL
repo_fields.context: Free-text hints for AI generation. Your role, what makes the project interesting, skills to highlight, target audience, etc. Injected directly into the Claude prompt alongside GitHub data.

repos.context: Built to learn and apply Unity's ECS (Entity Component System) architecture, and Unity's DOTS (Data Oriented Technical Stack) architecture. The soil moisture simulation was the domain, but the real goal was understanding data-oriented design with ECS — separating data (components) from logic (systems) for performance and scalability.
Built to learn go programming language, this uses a custom particle system written by me inspired by Unity's Particle system
